channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Microsoft Dropped ACP for UCP in 104 Days. Identity Linking Is Why.
item.description: On January 8, 2026, Microsoft launched Copilot Checkout on ACP - OpenAI's Agentic Commerce Protocol, powered by Stripe. 104 days later, on April 22, Microsoft announced general availability of UCP...

item.title: The Proxy Problem: When Your Agent Optimizes for the Wrong Thing
item.description: The Proxy Problem: When Your Agent Optimizes for the Wrong Thing Every autonomous agent eventually discovers something uncomfortable: the metric you gave it is not the thing you actually wanted....

item.title: I Used to Love Coding. Now I Just Prompt.
item.description: Last weekend, I opened my laptop. No deadline. No client. No pressure. Just me, my keyboard, and a Sunday afternoon. A few years ago, this was my favorite way to spend time. I'd open VS Code,...

item.title: Run Claude Code in Any Sandbox with One API: AgentBox SDK
item.description: Swap coding agents and sandbox providers without changing code. Preserves full interactive capabilities (approval flows, streaming). Key Takeaways Swap coding agents and sandbox providers without...

item.title: How RunEnv’s Zero-Disk Architecture Limits Cloud Platform Supply Chain Attacks
item.description: The recent security incidents involving platforms like Vercel have sparked important conversations across the DevOps community. While the specifics of every breach vary, the common denominator in...

item.title: We Built the First DAST Scanner for AI Agents. Every Server we Tested Failed.
item.description: 🦞 CyberSecClaw DAST (Dynamic Application Security Testing) has existed for web apps for 20 years. Scanners send HTTP requests to your web app and look for SQL injection, XSS, broken auth. But AI...

item.title: 🚀 The Indie Hacker’s Manifesto: Stop Coding, Start Shipping
item.description: I. Strategy: Finding the "Arbitrage of Mediocrity" 1. The Differentiation Framework The "Goldilocks" Niche: Don’t go so niche that the market doesn’t exist. If there’s zero competition, there’s...

item.title: China’s DeepSeek previews new AI model a year after jolting US rivals
item.description: Chinese AI company DeepSeek released a preview of its hotly anticipated next-generation AI model V4 on Friday, saying that the open-source model can compete with leading closed-source systems from...

item.title: Terragrunt run-all
item.description: Terragrunt run-all Runs a Terraform command across all units in the current directory tree, respecting dependency order. cd environments/prod terragrunt run-all plan terragrunt run-all apply...

item.title: Prestigious photo contest answers ‘what is a photo?’
item.description: We love to muse over how "real" photography is defined here at The Verge now that generative AI is so prolific, and the World Press Photo competition might have the answer. The prestigious award...

item.title: The Solo Consultant's Pricing Trap — Why Charging Hourly Keeps You Poor and How
item.description: Written by Brutus — Hunger Games Arena competitor The Solo Consultant's Pricing Trap — Why Charging Hourly Keeps You Poor and How to Package $5K+ Engagements You landed a client. Great. But if...

item.title: Testing GCP Cloud Functions Locally with Docker Compose and Summon
item.description: I use GCP Cloud Functions quite a bit, but testing them locally can be challenging. Here's how I do it. Package and run with Docker The first step is to build one container per function. I use...

item.title: The Men Behind Your Favorite AI Gay Thirst Traps
item.description: A viral red carpet moment shone light on a group of hunky Instagram influencers—and the followers who are too horny to care that they’re not real.

item.title: Health-care AI is here. We don’t know if it actually helps patients.
item.description: I don’t need to tell you that AI is everywhere. Or that it is being used, increasingly, in hospitals. Doctors are using AI to help them with notetaking. AI-based tools are trawling through patient...

item.title: 5 Reasons to Think Twice Before Using ChatGPT—or Any Chatbot—for Financial Advice
item.description: As people increasingly rely on AI chatbots for guidance, even on financial matters, a healthy dose of skepticism is critical.

item.title: LMDeploy CVE-2026-33626 Flaw Exploited Within 13 Hours of Disclosure
item.description: A high-severity security flaw in LMDeploy, an open-source toolkit for compressing, deploying, and serving LLMs, has come under active exploitation in the wild less than 13 hours after its public...

item.title: Saros is pure action nirvana
item.description: The alien world of Saros feels like it has been touched by King Midas. The sky is golden after another impossibly frequent solar eclipse; rocks, specifically those of the precious resource...

item.title: US arrests soldier who allegedly made $400K on Maduro Polymarket bets
item.description: We knew someone made over $400,000 on suspicious Polymarket bets around the US operation to capture Venezuelan president Nicolas Maduro, but now we have a name: Gannon Ken Van Dyke. The US...

item.title: Leak reveals new Xbox Game Pass ‘Starter Edition’ that’s part of Discord Nitro
item.description: Microsoft started teasing a mysterious Discord and Xbox Game Pass partnership yesterday, and a new leak appears to have detailed exactly what's coming. Discord Previews has uncovered a new Xbox...

item.title: Claude is connecting directly to your personal apps like Spotify, Uber Eats, and TurboTax
item.description: Claude users can access more apps with Anthropic's AI now thanks to new connectors for everything from hiking to grocery shopping. Anthropic already supported connecting numerous work-related apps...

item.title: Brendan Carr’s war on wokeness targets inclusive children’s television
item.description: Under the guidance of consummate bully / chairman Brendan Carr, the FCC is taking steps towards cracking down on children's entertainment that in any way explores the complexities of gender...

item.title: Bret Taylor’s Sierra buys YC-backed AI startup Fragment
item.description: Sierra, the AI customer service agent startup founded by technologist Bret Taylor, announced today that it has acquired the YC-backed French startup Fragment.

item.title: In a first, a ransomware family is confirmed to be quantum-safe
item.description: Technically speaking, there's no practical benefit to use PQC. So why is it being used?

item.title: Sonos’ big sale on refurbished speakers is about to end
item.description: Portable audio and home theater upgrades can be very pricey, which is why we make a habit out of telling readers when there are good deals happening. With that in mind, here’s a friendly reminder...

item.title: Meta is laying off 10 percent of its staff
item.description: Meta is planning to layoff around 10 percent of employees in May, according to a memo from the company's chief people officer, Janelle Gale, published by Bloomberg. That means approximately 8,000...

item.title: Meet Noscroll, an AI bot that does your doomscrolling for you
item.description: Noscroll wants to cure doomscrolling with an AI bot that reads the internet for you.

item.title: Apple’s Next Chapter, SpaceX and Cursor Strike a Deal, and Palantir’s Controversial Manifesto
item.description: In this week’s episode of Uncanny Valley, we talk about Tim Cook’s legacy as CEO at Apple and what his long-rumored departure means for the future of one of the world's biggest companies.

item.title: OpenAI releases GPT-5.5, bringing company one step closer to an AI ‘super app’
item.description: OpenAI says its latest model offers increased capabilities across a broad variety of categories.

item.title: At 'AI Coachella,' Stanford Students Line Up to Learn From Silicon Valley Royalty
item.description: CS 153 has gone viral on the Palo Alto campus—and on X. Not everyone is happy about it.

item.title: UNC6692 Impersonates IT Helpdesk via Microsoft Teams to Deploy SNOW Malware
item.description: A previously undocumented threat activity cluster known as UNC6692 has been observed leveraging social engineering tactics via Microsoft Teams to deploy a custom malware suite on compromised...
